title: Figma Variables & Modes
summary: Figma's first-party guide to variables, collections, modes, aliases, scopes, and team-library publication for reusable design decisions across themes and products.
lessons: Model theme and brand variants as modes on shared collections instead of duplicating token sets.
Use aliases to preserve semantic relationships between primitives and contextual values.
Publish and consume variables through explicit library boundaries with controlled scopes.
caveats: Figma's editor concepts and plan-specific library features are implementation evidence, not a universal runtime token format.
Verify exported code and platform mappings separately; a design variable does not prove production usage.
licenseNote: Public web documentation or standard; cite and synthesize.
antiImitationNote: Transfer principles and patterns; never copy brand identity, assets, or proprietary visuals.
